content.problemStatement:
Write a C program that generates and prints the fibonacci(n) using a recurssion  function. The function should take an integer n as an argument and returns the Fibonacci to the nth term.

**Note:** The **Fibonacci sequence** F0,F1,…FnF_0, F_1, \ldots F_nF0​,F1​,…Fn​ is a special infinite sequence of non-negative integers, where F0=0F_0 = 0F0​=0, F1=1F_1 = 1F1​=1 and for each integer n≥2n \ge 2n≥2, Fn=Fn−1+Fn−2F_n = F_{n-1} + F_{n-2}Fn​=Fn−1​+Fn−2​.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N denoting the term of the Fibonacci sequence to compute.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the Nth Fibonacci number on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are three test cases. N=6 → Fibonacci(6)=8. N=9 → Fibonacci(9)=34. N=1 → Fibonacci(1)=1.
Two test cases. N=2 → Fibonacci(2)=1. N=3 → Fibonacci(3)=2.
Single test case with the maximum allowed N. Fibonacci(30)=832040.

solution.approach: The Fibonacci numbers satisfy the recurrence F(n)=F(n-1)+F(n-2) with base cases F(0)=0 and F(1)=1. A naive recursive solution recomputes the same sub‑problems many times and becomes exponential. By storing already computed values in a memoisation array (top‑down DP) each value is computed only once, giving a linear time algorithm. For each test case we reset the memo array, compute F(N) using the recursive function with memoisation and print the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N ≤ 30, so the total is O(T·N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the memoisation array and recursion stack (depth ≤ N).

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{static int fib(int n,int[] memo){if(n<=1)return n;if(memo[n]!=-1)return memo[n];memo[n]=fib(n-1,memo)+fib(n-2,memo);return memo[n];}public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int[] memo=new int[31];for(int tc=0;tc<T;tc++){int n=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());Arrays.fill(memo,-1);int ans=fib(n,memo);sb.append(ans);if(tc!=T-1)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
